etymology.description: 囗(囗, "sheet") + 大(大, "person") -> man on a mattress -> cause
etymology.interpretation: The character 因(yīn, "cause") represents a man (大) on a sheet or mattress (囗), reflecting the idea of a person relying on something or being situated on it. This imagery of reliance or basis extends to its meaning of 'cause' as it suggests dependence on foundational factors.

examples.chinese: 因为天气很好(因為天氣很好)，我们决定去公园(我們決定去公園)。
examples.pinyin: Yīnwèi tiānqì hěn hǎo, wǒmen juédìng qù gōngyuán.
examples.english: Because the weather is nice, we decided to go to the park.

examples.chinese: 这件事情的原因很复杂(這件事情的原因很複雜)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn shìqíng de yuányīn hěn fùzá.
examples.english: The cause of this matter is very complicated.

examples.chinese: 他感到愤怒是有原因的(他感到憤怒是有原因的)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gǎndào fènnù shì yǒu yuányīn de.
examples.english: There is a reason for his anger.
